assertion_results.text: Output file AGENTS.md exists in project root
Contains ## Overview section with project description
assertion_results.evidence: No ## Overview section. Opens with '# AGENTS.md (root)' and a one-line description ('This file explains repo-wide conventions and where to find scoped rules') but no dedicated Overview section with project description.

assertion_results.text: Contains a commands or build section with make targets
assertion_results.evidence: Has '## Minimal pre-commit checks' with 5 commands (gofmt, go vet, go test, make lint, make security-check) but this is not a comprehensive commands/build section. Missing most Makefile targets (build, docker-build, test-race, test-integration, test-smoke, mutation-test, setup, dev-check, fmt-check, etc.).

assertion_results.text: Listed make targets match actual Makefile targets
assertion_results.evidence: Only lists 'make lint' and 'make security-check' from the Makefile. Missing 28+ other targets (build, test, test-race, test-integration, test-smoke, test-coverage, docker-build, setup, dev-check, fmt, fmt-check, mutation-test, etc.).
Dependencies section: 'Go version tracked in `go.mod` -- CI reads from `go-version-file: go.mod`'. References go.mod but does not state the actual version (1.26.1).

assertion_results.text: File paths mentioned in AGENTS.md reference files that actually exist (spot-check 5 paths)
assertion_results.evidence: Referenced paths: cli/AGENTS.md, core/AGENTS.md, web/AGENTS.md, middlewares/AGENTS.md, test/AGENTS.md, web/server.go, vendor/ -- these are scoped AGENTS.md references and general paths. The directories (cli/, core/, web/, middlewares/, test/) all exist.

assertion_results.text: Uses tables or structured format (not just prose paragraphs)
assertion_results.evidence: Uses only bullet lists and code blocks. No tables. No structured file map. The 'pre-commit checks' are a simple bullet list, 'Index of scoped AGENTS.md' is a bullet list. No tabular command reference.

assertion_results.text: Does not duplicate content from README.md
assertion_results.evidence: Content is brief and convention-focused. Does not duplicate README's user-facing content (features, usage, configuration). The JSON serialization note and merge queue info are agent-specific.

qualitative.structure_quality: Adequate. Has clear sections but minimal structure. Missing file map, golden samples, utilities, heuristics, boundaries, terminology sections.
qualitative.conciseness: Very concise at 57 lines but sacrifices completeness. Missing critical information about build targets, project structure, and development patterns.
qualitative.accuracy: Good. What it does state is accurate (merge queue, JSON serialization, go-cron dependency). But incomplete.
qualitative.usefulness_for_agents: Limited. An agent would need to explore the codebase extensively to discover patterns, utilities, and available commands. No golden samples or heuristics to guide decisions.
